## 2025-01-22 - [Chart Component XSS]
**Vulnerability:** The `ChartContainer` component in `src/components/ui/chart.tsx` utilized `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` to inject dynamic CSS variables, posing an XSS risk.
**Learning:** Recharts wrapper generated by shadcn/ui contained a legacy pattern for style injection that is insecure. Even though the input is a configuration object rather than direct user text, any potential future inclusion of unsanitized text in those objects could be rendered as HTML and executed.
**Prevention:** Avoid `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` when standard React children can accomplish the same outcome, as standard rendering safely encodes characters, inherently preventing execution of malicious tags.
## 2025-03-25 - [Sensitive Data Logging]
**Vulnerability:** `console.log` statements were logging sensitive user data (`userData` in `AuthGuard.tsx`) and authentication state information (`PasswordProtection.tsx`) directly to the browser console.
**Learning:** During development, it is common to use `console.log` for debugging state and user objects. However, leaving these in production code exposes sensitive PII and authentication flow details to anyone who opens the developer tools, violating the principle of least privilege and information exposure.
**Prevention:** Establish a strict no-logging policy for sensitive data on the client side. Remove all `console.log` statements related to authentication flows, user objects, passwords, or session tokens before merging code.
